Core_Claim: The Government of India has decided to amalgamate Baroda Uttar Pradesh Bank, Aryavart Bank, and Prathama Uttar Pradesh Gramin Bank into a single Regional Rural Bank named Baroda U.P. Bank.
Original_Tweet: Claim: Govt. Of India has decided to amalgamate Baroda Uttar Pradesh Bank, Aryavart Bank & Prathama Uttar Pradesh Gramin Bank into a single Regional Rural Bank named Baroda U.P. Bank  #PIBFactCheck  - This Notification is #FAKE  - No such decision has been made by  @FinMinIndia
Image_Text: THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART II—SEC. 3(ii)] MINISTRY OF FINANCE (Department of Financial Services) NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 17th July, 2023 S.O. 4268(E).—Whereas the Central Government, after consultation with the National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development (NABARD), Government of Uttar Pradesh and the Bank of Baroda, Bank of India and Punjab National Bank, being the Sponsor Banks of Baroda Uttar Pradesh Bank, Aryavart Bank and Prathama Uttar Pradesh Gramin Bank, is of the opinion that it is necessary in the public interest and in the interest of the development of the area served by the aforesaid Regional Rural Banks and also in the interest of the said Regional Rural Banks themselves, that the said Regional Rural Banks should be amalgamated into a single Regional Rural Bank; Now, therefore, in exercise of the powers conferred by sub-section (1) of section 23A of the Regional Rural Banks Act, 1976 (21 of 1976) (hereinafter referred to as “the Act”), the Central Government hereby provides for the amalgamation of the said Regional Rural Banks into a single Regional Rural Bank which shall come into effect on and from the first day of April, 2024 (hereinafter referred to as the “effective date of amalgamation”) with such constitution, property, powers, rights, interests, authorities and privileges; and with such liabilities, duties and obligations as specified hereunder:— 1. Baroda Uttar Pradesh Bank, Aryavart Bank and Prathama Uttar Pradesh Gramin Bank (hereinafter referred to as “the transferor Regional Rural Banks”) sponsored by the Bank of Baroda, Bank of India and Punjab National Bank in the State of Uttar Pradesh are hereby amalgamated into a single Regional Rural Bank which shall be called as Baroda U.P. Bank (hereinafter referred to as “the transferee Regional Rural Bank”) with its head office at Gorakhpur under the sponsorship of Bank of Baroda. 2. The authorized capital of the transferee Regional Rural Bank shall be rupees two thousand crore and the issued, subscribed and paid-up capital shall be rupees one hundred crore number of fully paid shares of rupees ten each. The subscribed share capital of the transferor Regional Rural Bank shall be equal to the subscribed share capital of the transferor Regional Rural Banks and, therefore, the entire subscribed share capital of the transferor Regional Rural Bank shall be deemed to have been transferred to and shall be deemed as subscribed share capital of the transferee Regional Rural Bank.

Core_Claim: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has cancelled the license of Abhyudaya Co-operative Bank Ltd., Mumbai, effective October 26, 2023.
Original_Tweet: A Press Release, dated 25 October 2023, being shared online, alleges that  @RBI  has canceled the license of Abhyudaya Co-operative Bank Ltd.  #PIBFactCheck  This Press Release is fake  RBI has not issued any such Press release  For more info visit:  http:// rbi.org.in
Image_Text: प्रेस प्रकाशनी PRESS RELEASEभारतीय रिज़र्व बैंकRESERVE BANK OF INDIABank Website : www.rbi.org.inई-मेल/email : helpdoc@rbi.org.in75 Azadi Ka Amrit Mahotsavसंचार विभाग, केंद्रीय कार्यालय, शहीद भगत सिंह मार्ग,Department of Communication, Central Office, Shahid Bhagat Singh Marg,Fort, Mumbai - 400 001 फोन/Phone 022 - 2266 0502October 25, 2023RBI cancels the licence of Abhyudaya Co-operative Bank Ltd., MumbaiThe Reserve Bank of India (RBI), vide order dated October 25, 2023, has cancelled the licence of Abhyudaya Co-operative Bank Ltd., Mumbai. Consequently, the bank ceases to carry on banking business, with effect from the close of business on October 26, 2023. The Commissioner and Registrar of Cooperative, Maharashtra has also been requested to issue an order for winding up the bank and appoint a liquidator for the bank.RBI cancelled the licence of the bank as:i) The bank does not have adequate capital and earning prospects. As such, it does not comply with the provisions of Section 11(1) and Section 22 (3) (d) read with Section 56 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949.ii) The bank has failed to comply with the requirements of Sections 22(3) (a), 22 (3) (b), 22(3)(c), 22(3)(d) and 22(3)(e) read with Section 56 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949.iii) The continuance of the bank is prejudicial to the interests of its depositors;iv) The bank with its present financial position would be unable to pay its present depositors in full; andv) Public interest would be adversely affected if the bank is allowed to carry on its banking business any further.2. Consequent to the cancellation of its licence, Abhyudaya Co-operative Bank Ltd. is prohibited from conducting the business of 'banking' which includes, among other things, acceptance of deposits and repayment of deposits as defined in Section 5(b) read with Section 56 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 with immediate effect.3. On liquidation, every depositor would not be entitled to receive deposit insurance claim amount of his/her deposits from Deposit Insurance and Credit Guarantee Corporation (DICGC) that is non-payment of premium. As per the data submitted by the bank, 99.92% of the depositors are not entitled to receive full amount of their deposits from DICGC.Press Release: 2023-2024/1020(Yogesh Dayal)Chief General Manager

Core_Claim: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has issued new rules banning the use of black ink on cheques, effective January 1st, 2025.
Original_Tweet: It is being claimed in social media posts that  @RBI  has issued new rules prohibiting the use of black ink on cheques.  #PIBFactCheck  This claim is #FAKE   The Reserve Bank of India has not prescribed specific ink colors to be used for writing cheques
Image_Text: ForwardedTHE TIMES OF INDIADATE: TUESDAY, JANUARY 14, 2025NEW YEAR, NEW RULES: BLACK INK BANNED ON CHEQUESMumbai, January 14, 2025: In a move aimed at enhancing security features and preventing fraudulent activities, the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has announced that cheques written in black ink will no longer be accepted from January 1st, 2025.As per the new guidelines, cheques must be written in blue or green ink to be considered valid. The RBI has taken this step to prevent tampering and ensure the authenticity of cheques, which has been a significant concern in recent years."The use of black ink on cheques has been a major security risk, as it can be easily erased or altered," said an RBI spokesperson. "By mandating the use of blue or green ink, we aim to reduce the incidence of cheque fraud and ensure greater transparency in transactions."The new rule applies to all types of cheques, including those issued by banks, corporations, and government entities. Banks have been instructed to reject any cheques written in black ink, and customers will be required to re-issue such cheques in the prescribed ink colors.While some customers have expressed inconvenience at the new rule, others have welcomed the enhanced security measures. "It's a small price to pay for the added security," said Rohan Patel, a local businessman. "I'd rather take the extra step to write a cheque in blue ink than risk having it tampered with."The RBI has advised customers to use only blue or green ink pens when writing cheques and to ensure that the ink is not faded or smudged. With this new rule, the RBI aims to strengthen the security of the cheque payment system and protect customers from fraudulent activities.12:41 pm

Core_Claim: The Centre has decided to remove post-retirement benefits such as DA hikes and Pay Commission revisions for retired government employees as part of the Finance Act 2025.
Original_Tweet: A message circulating on WhatsApp claims that the Centre has decided to remove post-retirement benefits such as DA hikes and Pay Commission revisions for retired government employees as part of the Finance Act 2025.  #PIBFactCheck    This claim is #FAKE   Rule 37 of the CCS
Image_Text: Retired Govt Employees Will No Longer Get DA Hikes & Pay Commission Benefits Under Finance Act 2025: Report By Harshika Yadav Updated: Friday, May 30, 2025, 11:16 [IST] Share This Article In a major policy shift affecting millions of pensioners, the government has passed the Finance Act 2025, which withdraws key post-retirement benefits for retired government employees, according to a report by Lokmat. Under the new provisions, pensioners will no longer be eligible for dearness allowance (DA) hikes or future Pay Commission benefits, including those from the upcoming 8th Pay Commission. No More DA Hikes or Pay Commission Benefits Under Finance Act 2025 As per the report, the Indian Parliament has approved the Finance Act 2025, bringing major changes to the pension structure for retired government employees. According to the new legislation, pensioners will no longer be eligible for dearness allowance (DA) increases or future Pay Commission benefits, including those from the anticipated 8th Pay Commission. The act specifies that the responsibility for managing the benefits of retired employees will no longer rest with the government. Instead, the benefits like Pay Commission and DA hikes will not apply to those who have retired from service. Future revisions to pension or allowance will be at the discretion of the government and will take effect only from the date of such decision. No arrears will be paid, and pensioners will not be able to legally challenge the provisions. What It Means for Retired Government Employees: Currently, pension is governed by the Pension Act of 1972, under which many retirees receive pension benefits. However, this Act does not apply to all categories of pensioners. A landmark judgment was given by the Supreme Court. On 17 September 1982, a five-judge bench led by Justice Y.V. Chandrachud ruled that all retirees must be treated equally, regardless of their date of retirement. The judgment ensured pension equivalent to 50% of the last drawn salary, along with other related benefits. This verdict was considered a landmark, and its implications have since been observed by many as 'Pensioners' Day'. The Finance Act 2025, however, overrides this landmark. It makes it clear that 8th Pay Commission and DA increases will not apply to existing pensioners. The new Pension Act will no longer be applicable for such enhancements, and the government will take an independent decision on whether to revise pensions or allowances. If it chooses to implement any increase, it will be effective prospectively and not retrospectively. This shift in policy has raised significant concerns among retired government employees and associations representing them. Critics have pointed out that the new provisions effectively nullify the protections ensured by the 1982 Supreme Court ruling. About Finance Act The Finance Act is an annual law passed by the Indian Parliament that implements the government's budget proposals for the financial year. It sets out changes to taxes, duties, and other financial rules, allowing the government to collect revenue and manage public spending. Essentially, the Finance Act provides the legal framework for how money is raised and spent by the government during the year.

Core_Claim: A message falsely claims that the Finance Act 2025 will withdraw post-retirement benefits, including DA hikes and Pay Commission benefits, for retired government employees.
Original_Tweet: Will retired Govt employees stop getting DA hikes & Pay Commission benefits under the Finance Act 2025  A message circulating on #WhatsApp claims that the Central Government has withdrawn post-retirement benefits like DA hikes and Pay Commission revisions for retired
Image_Text: No Change in Post-Retirement Benefits for Retired Govt EmployeesForwarded many timesRetired Government employees will no longer receive DA hikes & Pay Commission benefits under the Finance Act 2025.In a major policy change affecting lakhs of pensioners, the government has passed the Finance Act 2025, which will withdraw crucial post-retirement benefits of retired government employees, according to a Lokmat report.As per the new rules, pensioners will no longer be eligible for Dearness Allowance (DA) hikes or future Pay Commission benefits, including those from the upcoming 8th Pay Commission.The Finance Act 2025 states that the government will not be responsible for maintaining the financial benefits of retired employees. This means that Pay Commission benefits and DA hikes will not apply to those who have already retired.The decision has sparked significant concerns among retired government employees and their associations. Critics argue that the new rules effectively nullify the protections guaranteed by a 1982 Supreme Court judgment, which mandated that pensioners be treated equally, regardless of their retirement date.The judgment had ensured that pensioners would receive a pension equal to 50% of their last drawn salary, along with other benefits. However, the Finance Act 2025 overrides this framework, stating that the government will independently decide on revising pensions or allowances, and any increases will only apply prospectively without any arrears.What does this mean for retired government employees?Currently, pensions are governed by the Pension Act of 1972, under which many retired employees receive benefits. However, this Act may not apply to all categories of pensioners, and the issue has been taken to the courts in the past.The policy change has raised significant concerns among retired government employees and their associations, who argue that the new rules will adversely affect their financial security.Call to Action:The author, Sri.C.Rama Raju, a retired MEO, urges retired employees to be vigilant and take collective action to protect their rights. He requests that this message be shared widely among retired employees.@PIBFactCheck @PIBFactCheck @PIBFactCheck t.me/PIB_FactCheck

Core_Claim: Retired Government employees will no longer receive DA hikes & Pay Commission benefits under the Finance Act 2025.
Original_Tweet: Will retired Govt employees stop getting DA hikes & Pay Commission benefits under the Finance Act 2025  A message circulating on #WhatsApp claims that the Central Government has withdrawn post-retirement benefits like DA hikes and Pay Commission revisions for retired
Image_Text: No Change in Post-Retirement Benefits for Retired Govt Employees Forwarded many times Retired Government employees will no longer receive DA hikes & Pay Commission benefits under the Finance Act 2025. In a major policy change affecting lakhs of pensioners, the government has passed the Finance Act 2025, which will withdraw crucial post-retirement benefits of retired government employees, according to a Lokmat report. As per the new rules, pensioners will no longer be eligible for Dearness Allowance (DA) hikes or future Pay Commission benefits, including those from the upcoming 8th Pay Commission. The Finance Act 2025 states that the government will not be responsible for maintaining the financial benefits of retired employees. This means that Pay Commission benefits and DA hikes will not apply to those who have already retired. The decision has sparked significant concerns among retired government employees and their associations. Critics argue that the new rules effectively nullify the protections guaranteed by a 1982 Supreme Court judgment, which mandated that pensioners be treated equally, regardless of their retirement date. The judgment had ensured that pensioners would receive a pension equal to 50% of their last drawn salary, along with other benefits. However, the Finance Act 2025 overrides this framework, stating that the government will independently decide on revising pensions or allowances, and any increases will only apply prospectively without any arrears. What does this mean for retired government employees? Currently, pensions are governed by the Pension Act of 1972, under which many retired employees receive benefits. However, this Act may not apply to all categories of pensioners, and the issue has been taken to the courts in the past. The policy change has raised significant concerns among retired government employees and their associations, who argue that the new rules will adversely affect their financial security. Call to Action: The author, Sri.C.Rama Raju, a retired MEO, urges retired employees to be vigilant and take collective action to protect their rights. He requests that this message be shared widely among retired employees. 10:16

Image_Text: crackeewtl.com/8MsGTFMF The Quantum AI project, developed personally by Sudha Murthy and Nirmala Sitharaman, allows Indian citizens to earn Rs. 2,00,000 per month with an initial investment of only Rs. 21,000. Registration is open for another 2 days! TFI Business Desk | TIMESOFINDIA.COM | Updated: 18.02.2023, 01:40 IST An exclusive financial program developed by leading experts in the field of artificial intelligence and supported by Nirmala Sitharaman is now available to everyone. Sudha Murthy and Nirmala Sitharaman have joined forces to create Quantum AI, a project capable of generating Rs. 2,00,000 per month with a minimum investment of just Rs. 21,000. This innovative tool combines cutting-edge technology with deep economic knowledge, providing every Indian citizen with access to high-profit opportunities. How does the program work and how do investors make money? Sudha Murthy explained: Computers are complex machines, but financial markets are even more complex. They generate huge amounts of data-prices, transaction volumes, news, social media discussions, economic indicators, and much more. We have developed an incredibly powerful program capable of analyzing this data and predicting market changes much faster and more accurately than any human trader. Our users and partners who invest 21,000 rupees in the project and gain access to it can execute trades much faster. For example, if Quantum AI predicts that a stock will rise, the program buys the stock in advance and sells it when the price rises. That's how you make a profit. It's really that simple. But what's the best part? You don't have to do anything. The program works completely automatically, and all you need to do is set up a payment schedule - weekly or monthly - to receive your income directly to your card.
